models.kpiSummary: Compact KPI metrics displayed at the top of the Toolbox page.
models.aiModels: Open-source AI models developed during the program, each with GitHub repository.
models.studies: Research documents and analyses produced by partners like Briter.
models.bestPractices: Best practices reports from each hackathon event.
models.finalReport: The comprehensive final program report from Digital Africa.

relationships: AI Models are linked to the Hackathon where they were developed
AI Models are tagged with Sub-category and Country for filtering

filterOptions._note: Sectors and countries are now in product/shared/sectors.json and product/shared/countries.json

aiModels.name: AgriYield Predictor
aiModels.shortDescription: ML model predicting crop yields from weather and soil data.
aiModels.fullDescription: Machine learning model for predicting crop yields based on weather patterns, soil composition, and historical harvest data across Sub-Saharan Africa. Trained on 5 years of agricultural data from Kenya covering maize, wheat, and sorghum.
aiModels.useCase: Helps farmers and agricultural cooperatives plan planting schedules and optimize resource allocation based on predicted yields for key staple crops.

aiModels.name: CassavaBlight Detector
aiModels.shortDescription: Computer vision model detecting cassava mosaic and brown streak disease from leaf photos.
aiModels.fullDescription: Deep learning image classifier trained on 50,000+ cassava leaf images from Nigerian farms. Detects Cassava Mosaic Disease (CMD) and Cassava Brown Streak Disease (CBSD) with 94% accuracy from smartphone photos.
aiModels.useCase: Smallholder cassava farmers can diagnose plant diseases early by photographing leaves, enabling timely intervention before crop loss spreads.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.9+, TensorFlow Lite, mobile-optimized, works offline

aiModels.name: HarvestCredit Scorer
aiModels.shortDescription: Alternative credit scoring using farm productivity and mobile money patterns.
aiModels.fullDescription: Credit scoring model combining satellite-derived farm productivity indices with mobile money transaction history. Provides creditworthiness assessments for smallholder farmers without formal banking records in Ghana.
aiModels.useCase: Microfinance institutions and agri-lenders can assess loan eligibility for smallholder farmers using non-traditional data sources.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.9+, scikit-learn, pandas, satellite imagery APIs

aiModels.name: TeaLeaf Optimizer
aiModels.shortDescription: Precision harvesting model for tea plantations using weather and growth patterns.
aiModels.fullDescription: Machine learning model that optimizes tea leaf harvesting schedules by analyzing growth stage, weather conditions, and altitude data across Rwandan highland plantations. Maximizes leaf quality grades.
aiModels.useCase: Tea cooperatives can time their harvests to maximize premium-grade leaf yield, increasing revenue per hectare.

aiModels.name: SahelWater Monitor
aiModels.shortDescription: IoT-driven soil moisture and groundwater prediction for Sahel irrigation.
aiModels.fullDescription: Predictive model combining IoT soil moisture sensors with satellite precipitation data to forecast groundwater availability in the Sahel region. Provides 2-week advance irrigation planning for dry-season farming.
aiModels.useCase: Irrigated farming operations in Senegal's Sahel belt can optimize water use and plan pump schedules around predicted availability.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.9+, TensorFlow, compatible with Arduino/ESP32 sensors

aiModels.name: FarmRoute Optimizer
aiModels.shortDescription: Last-mile logistics optimization for perishable farm produce delivery.
aiModels.fullDescription: Route optimization algorithm designed for transporting perishable agricultural goods from rural farms to urban markets in Nigeria. Accounts for road conditions, traffic, and produce shelf-life constraints.
aiModels.useCase: Agricultural aggregators and logistics companies can reduce post-harvest losses by optimizing delivery routes for time-sensitive produce.

aiModels.name: CropDoc Vision
aiModels.shortDescription: Computer vision for plant disease detection across African crop varieties.
aiModels.fullDescription: Image classification model that identifies crop diseases from smartphone photos. Trained on African crop varieties including maize, beans, tomatoes, and coffee with region-specific disease profiles for East Africa.
aiModels.useCase: Farmers can diagnose plant diseases early by taking photos with their smartphones, reducing reliance on extension workers.

aiModels.name: CropInsure Risk
aiModels.shortDescription: Parametric crop insurance risk assessment using satellite and weather data.
aiModels.fullDescription: Risk assessment model for parametric crop insurance products. Combines satellite vegetation indices (NDVI) with weather station data to calculate payout triggers for drought and flood events in Ghana.
aiModels.useCase: Insurance providers can offer affordable, index-based crop insurance to smallholder farmers with automated payout mechanisms.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.9+, scikit-learn, satellite imagery APIs, weather station data

aiModels.name: AgriPrice Forecast
aiModels.shortDescription: Market price prediction for agricultural commodities in West African markets.
aiModels.fullDescription: Time series forecasting model for agricultural commodity prices across Nigerian markets. Covers yam, cassava, maize, and rice with 2-week price predictions based on supply data, seasonality, and trade flows.
aiModels.useCase: Farmers and traders can make informed decisions about when and where to sell crops based on predicted market prices.

aiModels.name: FarmerCredit Score
aiModels.shortDescription: Digital credit scoring for Rwandan farmers using cooperative transaction data.
aiModels.fullDescription: Credit scoring model leveraging farmer cooperative membership data, harvest delivery records, and mobile money history to generate credit scores for farmers in Rwanda's organized cooperative system.
aiModels.useCase: Agricultural banks and SACCOs can extend credit to cooperative members based on verifiable farming activity data.

aiModels.name: SoilSense Advisor
aiModels.shortDescription: Soil health analysis and fertilizer recommendation from sensor data.
aiModels.fullDescription: Precision agriculture model that interprets soil sensor readings (pH, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium) and combines them with crop requirements to generate field-specific fertilizer recommendations for Kenyan farms.
aiModels.useCase: Farmers and agronomists can get data-driven fertilizer prescriptions that optimize input costs and reduce over-application.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.8+, scikit-learn, compatible with common soil sensors

aiModels.name: SolarIrrigation Predict
aiModels.shortDescription: Solar pump output forecasting for off-grid irrigation systems.
aiModels.fullDescription: Forecasting model for solar-powered irrigation pump output in the Sahel region. Predicts daily water delivery volumes based on weather forecasts and solar irradiance data to help farmers plan irrigation schedules.
aiModels.useCase: Solar irrigation operators can schedule watering cycles around predicted pump capacity, reducing crop water stress.

aiModels.name: IrrigationFlow AI
aiModels.shortDescription: Smart irrigation scheduling for Nile Delta canal systems.
aiModels.fullDescription: AI-driven irrigation scheduling model for Egypt's Nile Delta canal network. Optimizes water distribution across farm plots using real-time flow sensor data, crop water requirements, and evapotranspiration forecasts.
aiModels.useCase: Water user associations and irrigation districts can reduce water waste and ensure equitable distribution across farming communities.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.8+, PyTorch, real-time sensor data feeds

aiModels.name: AgriGuard FraudShield
aiModels.shortDescription: Fraud detection for agricultural input subsidies and mobile lending.
aiModels.fullDescription: Real-time fraud detection system for agricultural subsidy disbursement and mobile agricultural lending platforms in Egypt. Identifies ghost beneficiaries, duplicate claims, and suspicious loan patterns.
aiModels.useCase: Government subsidy programs and agri-lenders can protect against fraud while ensuring legitimate farmers receive support.

aiModels.name: CattleHealth Monitor
aiModels.shortDescription: Wearable sensor analytics for early cattle disease detection.
aiModels.fullDescription: Analytics model processing data from low-cost cattle wearable sensors (temperature, activity, rumination) to detect early signs of East Coast Fever, trypanosomiasis, and mastitis in Rwandan dairy herds.
aiModels.useCase: Dairy farmers and veterinary services can detect cattle illness 2-3 days before visible symptoms, enabling early treatment.

aiModels.name: LivestockHealth Vision
aiModels.shortDescription: Computer vision for livestock disease detection in pastoral herds.
aiModels.fullDescription: Image-based disease detection for cattle, sheep, and goats in Sahel pastoral systems. Identifies Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR), foot-and-mouth disease, and skin conditions from smartphone photos.
aiModels.useCase: Pastoralists and community animal health workers can screen herds for disease during transhumance without veterinary lab access.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.8+, TensorFlow Lite, mobile-optimized, works offline

aiModels.name: GrainMarket Connect
aiModels.shortDescription: Supply-demand matching platform for grain trading across Southern Africa.
aiModels.fullDescription: Matching algorithm connecting grain producers with buyers across Southern African markets. Uses real-time price data, quality grades, transport costs, and storage availability to optimize trade flows for maize, wheat, and sorghum.
aiModels.useCase: Grain traders and cooperatives can find optimal buyers, reducing price information asymmetry and post-harvest storage losses.

aiModels.name: WineFrost Predictor
aiModels.shortDescription: Frost early warning system for Western Cape vineyards and fruit orchards.
aiModels.fullDescription: Micro-climate frost prediction model for South Africa's Western Cape wine and fruit regions. Combines topographic data, weather station feeds, and satellite temperature readings to provide 48-hour frost alerts at farm level.
aiModels.useCase: Wine and fruit farmers can activate frost protection measures (fans, heaters, irrigation) ahead of damaging frost events.

aiModels.name: OliveYield Forecast
aiModels.shortDescription: Olive tree yield prediction using climate and phenological data.
aiModels.fullDescription: Yield forecasting model for Moroccan olive orchards. Integrates flowering stage observations, rainfall patterns, temperature stress indicators, and historical yield data to predict harvest volumes 3 months ahead.
aiModels.useCase: Olive oil cooperatives can plan processing capacity, labor needs, and forward sales contracts based on reliable yield forecasts.

aiModels.name: AgriRemit Predictor
aiModels.shortDescription: Predicting agricultural input purchase timing from diaspora remittance flows.
aiModels.fullDescription: Model predicting when rural farming households will receive diaspora remittances and convert them to agricultural input purchases. Helps input dealers and cooperatives in Morocco plan inventory and credit cycles.
aiModels.useCase: Agricultural input dealers can align stock availability with seasonal remittance-driven purchasing patterns.

aiModels.name: NileDrought Warning
aiModels.shortDescription: Early warning system for agricultural drought in the Nile Basin.
aiModels.fullDescription: Satellite-based drought prediction model for Egypt's agricultural regions. Provides 2-3 month advance warning using NDVI anomalies, soil moisture indices, and upstream Nile flow data for crop planning.
aiModels.useCase: Governments and farming communities can trigger drought response plans and adjust planting strategies before conditions worsen.

aiModels.name: LocustSwarm Alert
aiModels.shortDescription: Desert locust swarm trajectory prediction and early alert system.
aiModels.fullDescription: Predictive model tracking desert locust breeding conditions and swarm movement patterns across North Africa. Combines satellite vegetation data, wind patterns, and historical swarm data to forecast infestation risk for Moroccan farmland.
aiModels.useCase: National plant protection services and farmers can prepare spraying operations and crop protection measures before swarms arrive.
aiModels.technicalRequirements: Python 3.9+, TensorFlow, satellite and weather APIs

aiModels.name: PoultryHealth Screener
aiModels.shortDescription: Automated poultry flock health screening from audio and image analysis.
aiModels.fullDescription: Multi-modal AI model analyzing poultry house audio recordings and camera feeds to detect respiratory diseases (Newcastle, infectious bronchitis) and abnormal flock behavior in South African broiler and layer operations.
aiModels.useCase: Commercial poultry farmers can detect disease outbreaks hours earlier than manual inspection, reducing mortality and antibiotic use.

aiModels.name: MarketConnect Match
aiModels.shortDescription: Connecting smallholder farmers to buyers based on produce and location.
aiModels.fullDescription: Matching algorithm connecting smallholder farmers with buyers based on produce type, quality grades, quantity, location, and transport feasibility across Ghanaian agricultural markets.
aiModels.useCase: Agricultural marketplaces can efficiently match supply with demand, reducing middleman costs and post-harvest losses for farmers.

studies.title: AI Readiness in African Startups
studies.description: Comprehensive survey of AI adoption barriers and opportunities across 8 African countries.
keyFindings: 72% of startups lack access to quality training data
Cost of cloud computing is top barrier
Agriculture shows highest AI potential across all surveyed countries

studies.title: Data Governance Frameworks for African AI
studies.description: Analysis of data protection regulations and their impact on AI development across the continent.
keyFindings: Only 28 African countries have data protection laws
GDPR-style regulations emerging in key markets

studies.title: Women in African AI: Landscape Analysis
studies.description: Survey of women founders and practitioners in the African AI ecosystem.
keyFindings: Women represent 22% of AI practitioners in surveyed countries
Funding gap persists for women-led AI startups

studies.title: Open Source AI Models: Impact Assessment
studies.description: Evaluation of the adoption and impact of open-source AI models released through the program.
keyFindings: 24 models released with 15,000+ GitHub stars combined
Models adopted by 50+ African agri-tech startups

bestPractices.title: Nairobi AgriTech Hackathon Best Practices
highlights: Data collection partnerships with KALRO for crop and soil datasets
Mobile-first model deployment for farmer accessibility
Farmer feedback integration through field testing

bestPractices.title: Lagos FoodSystems Hackathon Best Practices
highlights: Cross-sector collaboration between farmers and tech teams

bestPractices.title: Accra AgriFinance Hackathon Best Practices
highlights: Mobile money and farm data partnerships for credit scoring
Bias testing for agricultural lending models
Regulatory sandbox approach with Bank of Ghana

finalReport.title: AI4Startups Program Final Report
finalReport.description: Comprehensive report on the AI4Startups initiative outcomes, learnings, and recommendations for future programs.
